So I wanted to tell you one thing quickly about generating ideas. There's a couple of things you can do to ideas when you have one headline or you have an idea or you're looking at something. So you can add to it. What can I add to the idea? What can I, like we were talking for yours about, just do all of them, put them all together, add to it, bring other stuff in. What do you do it without? How much more stuff can you put in there? But then sometimes you want to try subtracting. What if I took some of this out? What if I only focused? What if I didn't tell them they were going to get wealthy? What if I didn't tell them they were going to save their life? What if I only focused on just the antibiotic part and just sold them? There's like a breakthrough in antibiotics. Finally, it's not disease resistant anymore. What if you divide? So instead of having one thing like this, you divide it up like that and you make it into separate things. Maybe you divide it, you put it in the copy. Maybe you make different ads instead of one ad from it. You divide it into lots of different ads. Don't just try to say the same thing in there. And then this is huge. Opposite. This is so powerful. Do the opposite of what you're doing. Like you could say, instead of how to make money in the stock market, what if we said how to lose money in the stock market? Here are all the ways to lose money in the stock market. You only invest in one region. You don't invest in different regions. You only invest in one region. So when that goes down, you lose money. Another way to lose money is not have good advice. Another way to lose money is to put all your investments, I don't know, in one place. We were talking earlier, I think, about the idea of in terms of gold instead of saying here's why you should invest in gold. Here's why you should not invest in gold. You should not invest in gold if you do it the wrong way, where you could lose money, where it's risky. I'll show you the right way to invest in gold. There was a thing that Arthur Johnson did for a newsletter. You know how, and it was kind of like he said, are you fed up? Everybody's telling you to drink more water, and everybody's telling you not to eat meat, and everybody's telling you not to smoke cigarettes, everybody's telling you not to drink coffee. And he said, he did the opposite. He said, here's why coffee is good for you. Here's why drinking too much water is bad for you. All these things are the opposite. I'm going to tell you how to do unhealthy things and benefit. So sometimes just the thought experiment of how can I do. So when you're doing 100 headlines and you do 10 headlines, maybe try to do the opposite of each one. Instead of how to be healthy, how to be sick. I think people who are creative, I don't think they necessarily have better ideas. I think they just have more ideas. They may only have good ideas 10% of the time, but they do 100 ideas, so they have 10 really good ideas. Whereas I think some people only do 10 ideas, they only have one good idea. And if they do five ideas, they have no good idea. So don't stop at five. Don't stop at 10. Go to 100.
